Plugin.Details: Detects calls to Java regex evaluation methods that may compile patterns on each invocation.

Detector.Details: Detects calls to Pattern.compile, Pattern.matches, String.matches, String.replaceAll, String.replaceFirst, String.split, and Apache Commons RegExUtils methods. These calls implicitly or explicitly compile a regex pattern and are only permitted for reviewed regular expressions or known inputs.

BugCode.Description: String method implicitly compiles a regex pattern

BugPattern.LongDescription: Call to {0} uses the explicit regex Pattern API in {1}.
BugPattern.Details: Usage of Pattern.compile(...) and Pattern.matches(...) is only allowed for reviewed regular expressions or known inputs. Compiling a pattern at every call site incurs unnecessary overhead. Consider compiling the pattern once as a static constant, or confirm that the input is safe before merging.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: String method implicitly compiles a regex pattern
BugPattern.LongDescription: Call to {0} implicitly compiles a regex pattern on every invocation in {1}.
BugPattern.Details: Usage of String.matches(...), String.replaceAll(...), String.replaceFirst(...), and String.split(...) implicitly compile a regex pattern on each call. This is only allowed for reviewed regular expressions or known inputs. Consider using a pre-compiled Pattern constant instead.

BugPattern.LongDescription: Call to {0} uses Apache Commons RegExUtils in {1}.
BugPattern.Details: Usage of RegExUtils is only allowed for reviewed regular expressions or known inputs. Ensure that the regular expression passed to this method has been reviewed for correctness and safety.
